summary: A basic static website for a single-person landscaping company serving Marshall, Longview, Jefferson, and surrounding areas in Texas.
architectural_notes: Static HTML/CSS site with no backend or dynamic content
known_issues: 'greater metro area' text still appears at the top of the website (hero or header area)

existing_features.title: Create a single-page static website for a one-man landscaping company with the following sections: Hero (with company name, tagline, and call-to-action button), Services (list of common landscaping services), About (brief bio/description of the owner), and Contact (phone number, email, and service area).

Apply a cohesive visual theme inspired by nature and outdoor work: earthy greens, warm soil browns, and cream/off-white tones. Use a sturdy serif or slab-serif heading font paired with a clean sans-serif body font. Layout should feel grounded and trustworthy, with generous whitespace, subtle texture-like backgrounds, and bold section dividers.
Update all instances of the service area text in the Contact section and Footer to read 'Serving Marshall, Longview, Jefferson & Surrounding Areas, Texas' (or equivalent natural phrasing).

Find and remove or replace every occurrence of 'greater metro area' (or similar generic metro area phrasing) anywhere in the Navbar, Hero section, or any other component rendered at the top of the page, replacing it with 'Serving Marshall, Longview, Jefferson & Surrounding Areas, Texas'.

Change all white-colored text throughout the site to black. This includes any text using Tailwind classes like `text-white`, inline white color styles, or CSS custom properties that resolve to white. Apply this change across all components: Navbar, Hero, Services, About, Contact, Footer, and any other rendered components. Ensure readability is maintained by checking contrast against background colors — where a dark background would make black text unreadable, use a very dark near-black (e.g., `text-gray-900`) or adjust the background color to a light tone so black text remains legible.

In the Hero component, apply bold font weight to the tagline text 'Professional landscaping services with a personal touch. From lush lawns to stunning garden beds — I take pride in every blade of grass.' by adding the Tailwind class `font-bold` (or `font-semibold` if it better fits the existing typography scale) to the element rendering that text.

In the Hero component (frontend/src/components/Hero.tsx), remove the phrase ' From lush lawns to stunning garden beds — ' from the tagline text, so that it reads: 'Professional landscaping services with a personal touch. I take pride in every blade of grass.'

In the Hero component (frontend/src/components/Hero.tsx), find the trust indicator stat that reads '200 happy clients' (or '200+' / '200 Happy Clients' or any similar variant) and replace it with 'dozens of satisfied customers'. Update both the number/value and the label text so no reference to '200' or 'happy clients' remains.

new_feature_requests.summary: Static landing page with hero section, services, about, and contact info

Find and replace any remaining instances of 'greater metro area' (or similar generic metro area phrasing) in the Contact section and Footer with 'Serving Marshall, Longview, Jefferson & Surrounding Areas, Texas'.
Change all white text to black throughout the site
Make the hero tagline text 'Professional landscaping services with a personal touch. From lush lawns to stunning garden beds — I take pride in every blade of grass.' bold
Remove the phrase ' From lush lawns to stunning garden beds — ' from the hero tagline, leaving it as 'Professional landscaping services with a personal touch. I take pride in every blade of grass.'
Change '200 happy clients' to 'dozens of satisfied customers' in the website copy
